content.text:
Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```
I'll analyze the existing CLAUDE.md file and the codebase to suggest improvements. Let me first read the current CLAUDE.md and explore the codebase structure.

1→# Neuman Brain 2→ 3→
4→ 5→ 6→ 7→### Your Brain. Your Data. Your Device. 8→ 9→**A privacy-first, local-only platform for organizing your life and work without surrendering your data.** 10→ 11→[![MIT License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) 12→[![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.9-blue)](https://www.typescriptlang.org/) 13→[![React](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-19-61DAFB)](https://react.dev/) 14→[![Vite](https://img.shields.io/badge/Vite-7-646CFF)](https://vitejs.dev/) 15→[![Bundle Size](https://img.shields.io/badge/Bundle-117KB_gzip-green)](https://vitejs.dev/) 16→[![Tests](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tests-79_passing-success)](https://vitest.dev/) 17→ 18→**[Live Demo](https://brain.neuman.dev)** | **[Documentation](docs/)** | **[Report Bug](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues)** | **[Request Feature](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/discussions)** 19→ 20→
21→ 22→--- 23→ 24→## Table of Contents 25→ 26→- [About Neuman Brain](#about-neuman-brain) 27→ - [Why Neuman Brain?](#why-neuman-brain) 28→ - [The Platform Philosophy](#the-platform-philosophy) 29→ - [From the Founder](#from-the-founder) 30→- [Features](#features) 31→ - [Notes & Knowledge Management](#notes--knowledge-management) 32→ - [Task & Project Management](#task--project-management) 33→ - [Time Tracking](#time-tracking) 34→ - [Calendar & Scheduling](#calendar--scheduling) 35→ - [Dashboard & Widgets](#dashboard--widgets) 36→ - [AI Terminal](#ai-terminal) 37→- [Privacy & Security](#privacy--security) 38→- [Comparison with Alternatives](#comparison-with-alternatives) 39→- [Getting Started](#getting-started) 40→ - [For Users](#for-users) 41→ - [For Developers](#for-developers) 42→- [Architecture & Tech Stack](#architecture--tech-stack) 43→- [Roadmap](#roadmap) 44→- [Contributing](#contributing) 45→- [Business Model](#business-model) 46→- [Legal](#legal) 47→- [Acknowledgments](#acknowledgments) 48→- [Contact](#contact) 49→ 50→--- 51→ 52→## About Neuman Brain 53→ 54→### Why Neuman Brain? 55→ 56→**Neuman Brain exists because the tools that are supposed to help us stay organized have quietly become another source of chaos.** 57→ 58→Most people now live across a stack of subscriptions and tabs: one app for planning, another for ideas, another for tracking work, another for personal logistics, plus a handful of dashboards at the office. Each one promises clarity. Together, they fracture attention, duplicate effort, and leave your information scattered across systems you don't really control. 59→ 60→On top of that, many of those tools feel unfinished: new features layered over old problems, workflows that never quite get refined, and products that change direction faster than you can build trust in them. 61→ 62→**Neuman Brain is being built from the opposite starting point.** 63→ 64→Instead of chasing surface features, almost all of the work so far has gone into an enduring foundation: how information is stored, how it is kept private, how risk is handled, and how the system should behave before it grows. 65→ 66→### The Platform Philosophy 67→ 68→A few core principles define that foundation: 69→ 70→| Principle | Description | 71→|-----------|-------------| 72→| **Local-First Mindset** | Your information lives with you first, not locked behind someone else's subscription or infrastructure | 73→| **Ownership & Respect** | Your thoughts, plans, and records are not fuel for engagement metrics or growth strategies | 74→| **Honest Communication** | The platform is described as it truly is: early, foundation-heavy, and far from complete | 75→| **Calm by Design** | No artificial urgency, attention traps, or manipulative flows | 76→| **Built for Many Lives** | The same core feels natural whether you're running a household, a team, a business, or all three | 77→ 78→> **Neuman Brain is intentionally at the 1% mark** - most of the work so far has gone into building a durable foundation and ethical guardrails, so every future layer rests on something you can actually trust. 79→ 80→### From the Founder 81→ 82→> *"My name is Travis J. Neuman, and Neuman Brain started as a personal line in the sand about how our tools should treat us.* 83→> 84→> *I've spent my career in information technology and operations. I've supported a multi-location hospitality group as an IT manager and senior systems administrator. I currently work as a technical project manager in managed services for a global industrial technology environment.* 85→> 86→> *Neuman Brain is not a protest against any employer - past or present. It is a response to something larger: the way we, as individuals and professionals, are increasingly bound to sprawling subscription stacks and unfinished products.* 87→> 88→> *What I stand for with Neuman Brain is simple:* 89→> - *Tools that hold our lives together should be designed with respect, not manipulation* 90→> - *Our information should not be hostage to subscriptions and shifting business models* 91→> - *Foundations should be built carefully, even if that means progress looks slower from the outside* 92→> 93→> *I use this platform myself. If a decision wouldn't be acceptable for my own life, it doesn't belong in the product."* 94→> 95→> **- Travis J. Neuman** 96→ 97→**[Read the full story in the app](https://brain.neuman.dev)** (Click "About" in the footer) 98→ 99→--- 100→ 101→## Features 102→ 103→### Notes & Knowledge Management 104→ 105→**Status:** Shipped (55% Obsidian parity, 50% Notion parity) 106→ 107→| Feature | Status | Description | 108→|---------|--------|-------------| 109→| Rich Text Editor | ✅ Shipped | Lexical-powered editor with live preview | 110→| Markdown Mode | ✅ Shipped | Full markdown editing support | 111→| Folder Hierarchy | ✅ Shipped | Unlimited depth, drag-and-drop organization | 112→| Full-Text Search | ✅ Shipped | Cmd/Ctrl+K quick switcher | 113→| Auto-Save | ✅ Shipped | Debounced saves to IndexedDB | 114→| Slash Commands | ✅ Shipped | /heading, /list, /code, /quote, /table | 115→| Export to Markdown | ✅ Shipped | Download notes as .md files | 116→| Word/Character Count | ✅ Shipped | Live status bar updates | 117→| [[Wiki-links]] | 📋 Planned | Coming Q2 2026 | 118→| Graph View | 💡 Future | Visual knowledge connections | 119→ 120→--- 121→ 122→### Task & Project Management 123→ 124→**Status:** Shipped (98% Jira/Linear parity) 125→ 126→| Feature | Status | Description | 127→|---------|--------|-------------| 128→| Kanban Board | ✅ Shipped | Drag-and-drop tasks across columns | 129→| Custom Columns | ✅ Shipped | Fully customizable workflow stages | 130→| Subtasks | ✅ Shipped | Nested tasks with full properties | 131→| Task Dependencies | ✅ Shipped | "Blocked By" and "Blocks" relationships | 132→| List View | ✅ Shipped | Sortable columns, bulk operations | 133→| Due Dates | ✅ Shipped | Calendar integration | 134→| Priority Levels | ✅ Shipped | High/Medium/Low with visual indicators | 135→| Export to CSV | ✅ Shipped | Full data export | 136→| Gantt Charts | 📋 Planned | Timeline visualization | 137→ 138→**Key Capabilities:** 139→- Circular dependency prevention 140→- Completion tracking with progress bars 141→- Three-color badge system (green/blue/gray) 142→- Bulk select, status change, priority change, delete 143→- Card detail panel with rich editing 144→ 145→--- 146→ 147→### Time Tracking 148→ 149→**Status:** Shipped (74% Toggl parity) 150→ 151→| Feature | Status | Description | 152→|---------|--------|-------------| 153→| Timer Controls | ✅ Shipped | Start/stop/pause/resume | 154→| Sidebar Panel | ✅ Shipped | Toggl-style persistent timer | 155→| **Timer Tab** | ✅ Shipped | Full-featured Schedule page tab | 156→| Recent Entries | ✅ Shipped | 48-hour history with continue | 157→| Manual Entry | ✅ Shipped | Add retroactive time entries | 158→| Project Support | ✅ Shipped | Create, assign, filter by project | 159→| Daily/Weekly Stats | ✅ Shipped | Visual charts and summaries | 160→| Monthly Reports | ✅ Shipped | Heat map, project breakdown | 161→| Calendar View | ✅ Shipped | Month grid, color-coded by project | 162→| CSV Export | ✅ Shipped | RFC 4180 compliant | 163→| Bulk Operations | ✅ Shipped | Multi-select, delete, update | 164→| Billable Hours | 📋 Planned | Coming Phase 5 | 165→ 166→**Timer Tab Features (New!):** 167→- Synchronized with sidebar timer 168→- Weekly statistics chart with daily breakdown 169→- Today/weekly/daily average displays 170→- Recent entries grid with click-to-continue 171→ 172→--- 173→ 174→### Calendar & Scheduling 175→ 176→**Status:** Shipped (65% Google Calendar parity) 177→ 178→| Feature | Status | Description | 179→|---------|--------|-------------| 180→| Month/Week/Day Views | ✅ Shipped | Full calendar navigation | 181→| Event Creation/Editing | ✅ Shipped | Rich event details | 182→| Drag-and-Drop | ✅ Shipped | Reschedule events easily | 183→| Recurring Events | ✅ Shipped | Daily, weekly, monthly patterns | 184→| Multi-Day Events | ✅ Shipped | Visual representation | 185→| Event Reminders | ✅ Shipped | Browser notifications | 186→| ICS Import/Export | ✅ Shipped | Google Calendar compatible | 187→| Agenda View | ✅ Shipped | Next 30 days list | 188→| Conflict Detection | ✅ Shipped | Overlapping events warning | 189→| Task Integration | ✅ Shipped | Due dates on calendar | 190→| Day Detail Modal | ✅ Shipped | Click any day for full view | 191→ 192→--- 193→ 194→### Dashboard & Widgets 195→ 196→**Status:** Shipped (70% Notion Dashboard parity) 197→ 198→**44+ widgets across 5 categories:** 199→ 200→| Category | Widgets | Examples | 201→|----------|---------|----------| 202→| **Productivity** | 6 | TaskSummary, UpcomingEvents, RecentNotes, QuickAdd, Pomodoro | 203→| **Information** | 9 | Weather, HackerNews, Reddit, DevTo, GitHub, Quotes | 204→| **Utilities** | 14 | Calculator, QRCode, ColorPalette, Countdown, WorldClock | 205→| **Finance** | 3 | Crypto, Currency, StockMarket | 206→| **Media** | 1 | Unsplash (random beautiful images) | 207→ 208→**Widget System Features:** 209→- Drag-and-drop grid layout (react-grid-layout) 210→- Resizable widgets 211→- Widget settings/configuration 212→- Lazy loading for performance 213→- Two-tier storage persistence (IndexedDB + localStorage) 214→ 215→--- 216→ 217→### AI Terminal 218→ 219→**Status:** Shipped (8 AI providers, 200+ models) 220→ 221→| Provider | Models | Features | 222→|----------|--------|----------| 223→| OpenRouter | 200+ | Free tier (50 req/day) | 224→| Groq | LPU-powered | Ultra-fast inference | 225→| HuggingFace | Open-source | Community models | 226→| Mistral | European | GDPR-friendly | 227→| Google Gemini | 1M context | Long documents | 228→| OpenAI | GPT-4o, o1 | Latest models | 229→| Anthropic | Claude 3.5 | Sonnet & Opus | 230→| xAI | Grok | Real-time info | 231→| **DeepSeek** | R1, V3 | Cost-effective reasoning | 232→ 233→**Security Features:** 234→- AES-256-GCM encrypted API key storage 235→- Session-based password with expiry 236→- Keys never logged or transmitted 237→- Automatic provider fallback 238→- Usage tracking per provider 239→ 240→**Phantom Shell (Browser Terminal):** 241→- Full xterm.js terminal emulator 242→- Node.js in browser via WebContainers 243→- AI ghost commands with `/ai` prefix 244→- Live preview for dev servers 245→ 246→--- 247→ 248→## Privacy & Security 249→ 250→### Zero-Data-Host Architecture 251→ 252→| What | Where | You Control? | 253→|------|-------|--------------| 254→| Notes | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | 255→| Tasks | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | 256→| Calendar | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | 257→| Time Entries | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | 258→| Settings | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | 259→| API Keys | Your device (encrypted) | 100% | 260→ 261→### Privacy Guarantees 262→ 263→1. **No data EVER leaves your device** - Everything stays in IndexedDB (50GB+ capacity) 264→2. **Zero tracking** - No Google Analytics, no Facebook Pixel, no ads 265→3. **No account required** - Use anonymously with local storage 266→4. **No cloud dependencies** - No Firebase, no Google Drive, no third-party servers 267→5. **Full data export always available** - Export to .brain files anytime 268→6. **Open source** - Audit the code yourself 269→ 270→### Analytics (Optional) 271→ 272→We use **Cloudflare Web Analytics** for basic, anonymous site metrics: 273→- Privacy-focused (no cookies, no fingerprinting) 274→- GDPR/CCPA compliant 275→- IP addresses never stored 276→- No individual user tracking 277→ 278→**[Read full Privacy Policy](https://brain.neuman.dev)** (Click "Privacy & Terms" in the footer) 279→ 280→--- 281→ 282→## Comparison with Alternatives 283→ 284→| Feature | Neuman Brain | Notion | Obsidian | Monday.com | Toggl | 285→|---------|--------------|--------|----------|------------|-------| 286→| **Cost** | Free | $10-15/mo | Free (Sync $10) | $12-20/mo | Free-$18/mo | 287→| **Data Privacy** | Local-first | Cloud-only | Local files | Cloud-only | Cloud-only | 288→| **Platform** | Web, PWA | Web, apps | Desktop-first | Web, apps | Web, apps | 289→| **Offline** | Full | Limited | Full | No | Limited | 290→| **Notes + Tasks** | Integrated | Separate | Plugin-based | Weak notes | No notes | 291→| **Time Tracking** | Built-in | No | Plugin | Partial | Core | 292→| **Bundle Size** | 117KB | 2-4MB | Fast | 3MB+ | N/A | 293→| **Open Source** | Yes (MIT) | No | Source-available | No | No | 294→ 295→### Feature Parity 296→ 297→| Category | Competitor | Parity | 298→|----------|------------|--------| 299→| Notes | Obsidian | 55% | 300→| Notes | Notion | 50% | 301→| Tasks/PM | Jira/Linear | 98% | 302→| Time Tracking | Toggl | 74% | 303→| Calendar | Google Calendar | 65% | 304→| Dashboard | Notion | 70% | 305→ 306→--- 307→ 308→## Getting Started 309→ 310→### For Users 311→ 312→**No installation required!** Just visit: 313→ 314→**[https://brain.neuman.dev](https://brain.neuman.dev)** 315→ 316→Your data is stored locally in your browser. No account needed. 317→ 318→**Quick Start:** 319→1. Visit the app 320→2. Start creating notes, tasks, or events 321→3. Track your time with the sidebar timer or Timer tab 322→4. Explore the 44+ dashboard widgets 323→5. Export your data anytime (Settings → Backup & Sync) 324→ 325→--- 326→ 327→### For Developers 328→ 329→**Prerequisites:** 330→- Node.js 22+ (see `.node-version`) 331→- npm or pnpm 332→ 333→**Setup:** 334→ 335→```bash 336→# Clone repository 337→git clone https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase.git 338→cd system.knowledgebase 339→ 340→# Install dependencies 341→npm install 342→ 343→# Start development server 344→npm run dev 345→# Open http://localhost:5173 346→ 347→# Run tests 348→npm run test 349→ 350→# Type checking 351→npm run type-check 352→ 353→# Build for production 354→npm run build 355→``` 356→ 357→**Developer Workflow:** 358→ 359→We use a task-based workflow documented in `CLAUDE.md`: 360→ 361→1. **Before coding:** Create task file in `tasks/[feature-name].md` 362→2. **During work:** Update task file with progress 363→3. **Git hooks:** Pre-commit hook blocks commits without task files 364→4. **After work:** Add review section, archive completed tasks 365→ 366→**Key Files:** 367→- `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow guide with 12 core rules 368→- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Step-by-step checklist 369→- `.claude/templates/task-template.md` - Task file template 370→ 371→--- 372→ 373→## Architecture & Tech Stack 374→ 375→### Frontend 376→ 377→| Technology | Version | Purpose | 378→|------------|---------|---------| 379→| React | 19 | UI framework | 380→| TypeScript | 5.9 | Type safety (strict mode) | 381→| Vite | 7 | Build tool, HMR | 382→| Zustand | 5 | State management (1KB) | 383→| Tailwind CSS | 3 | Styling | 384→| Vitest | - | Testing | 385→ 386→### Data Storage (Local-First) 387→ 388→| Layer | Technology | Capacity | 389→|-------|------------|----------| 390→| Primary | IndexedDB | 50GB+ | 391→| Backups | .brain files | Unlimited | 392→| Auto-save | File System Access API | User folder | 393→ 394→### Performance 395→ 396→| Metric | Value | 397→|--------|-------| 398→| Bundle Size | 117KB gzipped | 399→| Initial Load | < 1s | 400→| Code Splitting | 9 feature-based chunks | 401→| Lazy Loading | Widgets, Calendar, Kanban, AI | 402→ 403→### Project Structure 404→ 405→``` 406→system.knowledgebase/ 407→├── src/ 408→│ ├── pages/ # Dashboard, Tasks, Notes, TimeTracking, Settings 409→│ ├── widgets/ # 44+ dashboard widgets 410→│ ├── components/ # Reusable UI components 411→│ ├── stores/ # Zustand state management 412→│ ├── services/ # Data persistence, backup, AI 413→│ └── types/ # TypeScript definitions 414→├── docs/ # Documentation (56 files) 415→├── tasks/ # Active development tasks 416→├── .archive/ # Completed tasks, historical docs 417→└── .claude/ # Automation tools, templates 418→``` 419→ 420→--- 421→ 422→## Roadmap 423→ 424→### Phase 0: Foundation ✅ Complete 425→- React/TypeScript rebuild 426→- Kanban, Calendar, Time Tracking 427→- Privacy-first local storage 428→- AI Terminal with 8+ providers 429→ 430→### Phase 1: Notes & PKM ✅ Complete 431→- Lexical rich text editor 432→- Folders, tags, search 433→- Slash commands, markdown 434→- Export to Markdown 435→ 436→### Phase 2: Stability & Polish (Current) 437→- Modal overflow fixes 438→- Timer Tab in Schedule page 439→- Calendar day detail modal 440→- Notes sidebar resize 441→ 442→### Phase 3: Habit Tracking & Gamification (Q1 2026) 443→- Daily habits with streaks 444→- XP, levels, badges 445→- Analytics and visualizations 446→ 447→### Phase 4: Advanced PM (Q2 2026) 448→- Gantt charts 449→- Resource management 450→- Billable time tracking 451→ 452→### Phase 5: Collaboration (Q2-Q3 2026) 453→- Real-time sync 454→- Comments & @mentions 455→- Shared workspaces 456→ 457→**[Full Roadmap](docs/product/feature_roadmap.md)** 458→ 459→--- 460→ 461→## Contributing 462→ 463→We welcome contributions! Neuman Brain is open source (MIT License). 464→ 465→### How to Contribute 466→ 467→1. **Report bugs:** [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues) 468→2. **Request features:** [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/discussions) 469→3. **Submit code:** Fork repo → Create feature branch → Submit PR 470→4. **Improve docs:** Documentation PRs are especially welcome! 471→ 472→### Development Guidelines 473→ 474→- **TypeScript:** Strict mode, no `any` types 475→- **Tests:** Add tests for new features (Vitest + Testing Library) 476→- **Code Style:** Follow existing patterns, run `npm run lint` 477→- **Documentation:** Update relevant docs in `/docs` 478→- **Task Files:** Create task file before coding (see CLAUDE.md) 479→ 480→**[See CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)** for detailed guidelines. 481→ 482→--- 483→ 484→## Business Model 485→ 486→### How is this free? 487→ 488→**No catch.** Neuman Brain has **zero hosting costs**: 489→- Static hosting (Cloudflare Pages free tier) 490→- No backend servers 491→- No database hosting fees 492→- Users store their own data locally 493→ 494→### Sustainability Plans 495→ 496→| Model | Description | Status | 497→|-------|-------------|--------| 498→| Donations | GitHub Sponsors, Open Collective | Planned | 499→| Client-side Pro | Advanced local features (your API key) | Future | 500→| Professional Services | Custom integrations, training | Available | 501→ 502→**Commitment:** Core functionality will **always be free**. 503→ 504→**[Full Business Model](docs/business/business_model.md)** 505→ 506→--- 507→ 508→## Legal 509→ 510→### Privacy Policy 511→ 512→We respect your privacy. All data stays on YOUR device. We use Cloudflare Web Analytics (privacy-focused, no cookies) for basic site metrics. 513→ 514→**[Read full Privacy Policy](https://brain.neuman.dev)** (Click "Privacy & Terms" in the footer) 515→ 516→### Terms & Conditions 517→ 518→Currently being drafted. Core principles: 519→- Your data, your control 520→- Full data portability 521→- No hidden agendas 522→ 523→### License 524→ 525→**MIT License** - Use for anything, including commercial projects. 526→ 527→See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for full terms. 528→ 529→--- 530→ 531→## Acknowledgments 532→ 533→### Built With 534→ 535→| Technology | Purpose | 536→|------------|---------| 537→| [React](https://react.dev/) | UI framework | 538→| [TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org/) | Type safety | 539→| [Vite](https://vitejs.dev/) | Build tool | 540→| [Zustand](https://github.com/pmndrs/zustand) | State management | 541→| [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/) | Styling | 542→| [Lexical](https://lexical.dev/) | Rich text editor | 543→| [Vitest](https://vitest.dev/) | Testing | 544→| [Leaflet](https://leafletjs.com/) | Maps | 545→| [Open-Meteo](https://open-meteo.com/) | Weather API | 546→ 547→### Inspired By 548→ 549→- **Notion** - All-in-one flexibility 550→- **Obsidian** - Local-first, powerful linking 551→- **Apple Notes** - Simplicity and speed 552→- **Linear** - Professional task management 553→- **Toggl** - Elegant time tracking 554→- **Habitica** - Gamification for productivity 555→ 556→--- 557→ 558→## Contact 559→ 560→| Channel | Link | 561→|---------|------| 562→| **Live App** | [brain.neuman.dev](https://brain.neuman.dev) | 563→| **GitHub** | [github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase) | 564→| **Issues** | [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues) | 565→| **Discussions** | [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/discussions) | 566→| **Email** | [brain@neuman.dev](mailto:brain@neuman.dev) | 567→ 568→--- 569→ 570→
571→ 572→### Star This Repo! 573→ 574→If you find Neuman Brain useful, please star the repository on GitHub! It helps others discover the project. 575→ 576→[![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase?style=social)](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/stargazers) 577→ 578→--- 579→ 580→**Made with care for privacy, productivity, and open source** 581→ 582→**Your data. Your device. Your brain.** 583→ 584→--- 585→ 586→*Last updated: November 26, 2025* 587→ 588→
589→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

file.content:
# Neuman Brain
### Your Brain. Your Data. Your Device. **A privacy-first, local-only platform for organizing your life and work without surrendering your data.** [![MIT License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) [![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.9-blue)](https://www.typescriptlang.org/) [![React](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-19-61DAFB)](https://react.dev/) [![Vite](https://img.shields.io/badge/Vite-7-646CFF)](https://vitejs.dev/) [![Bundle Size](https://img.shields.io/badge/Bundle-117KB_gzip-green)](https://vitejs.dev/) [![Tests](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tests-79_passing-success)](https://vitest.dev/) **[Live Demo](https://brain.neuman.dev)** | **[Documentation](docs/)** | **[Report Bug](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues)** | **[Request Feature](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/discussions)**
--- ## Table of Contents - [About Neuman Brain](#about-neuman-brain) - [Why Neuman Brain?](#why-neuman-brain) - [The Platform Philosophy](#the-platform-philosophy) - [From the Founder](#from-the-founder) - [Features](#features) - [Notes & Knowledge Management](#notes--knowledge-management) - [Task & Project Management](#task--project-management) - [Time Tracking](#time-tracking) - [Calendar & Scheduling](#calendar--scheduling) - [Dashboard & Widgets](#dashboard--widgets) - [AI Terminal](#ai-terminal) - [Privacy & Security](#privacy--security) - [Comparison with Alternatives](#comparison-with-alternatives) - [Getting Started](#getting-started) - [For Users](#for-users) - [For Developers](#for-developers) - [Architecture & Tech Stack](#architecture--tech-stack) - [Roadmap](#roadmap) - [Contributing](#contributing) - [Business Model](#business-model) - [Legal](#legal) - [Acknowledgments](#acknowledgments) - [Contact](#contact) --- ## About Neuman Brain ### Why Neuman Brain? **Neuman Brain exists because the tools that are supposed to help us stay organized have quietly become another source of chaos.** Most people now live across a stack of subscriptions and tabs: one app for planning, another for ideas, another for tracking work, another for personal logistics, plus a handful of dashboards at the office. Each one promises clarity. Together, they fracture attention, duplicate effort, and leave your information scattered across systems you don't really control. On top of that, many of those tools feel unfinished: new features layered over old problems, workflows that never quite get refined, and products that change direction faster than you can build trust in them. **Neuman Brain is being built from the opposite starting point.** Instead of chasing surface features, almost all of the work so far has gone into an enduring foundation: how information is stored, how it is kept private, how risk is handled, and how the system should behave before it grows. ### The Platform Philosophy A few core principles define that foundation: | Principle | Description | |-----------|-------------| | **Local-First Mindset** | Your information lives with you first, not locked behind someone else's subscription or infrastructure | | **Ownership & Respect** | Your thoughts, plans, and records are not fuel for engagement metrics or growth strategies | | **Honest Communication** | The platform is described as it truly is: early, foundation-heavy, and far from complete | | **Calm by Design** | No artificial urgency, attention traps, or manipulative flows | | **Built for Many Lives** | The same core feels natural whether you're running a household, a team, a business, or all three | > **Neuman Brain is intentionally at the 1% mark** - most of the work so far has gone into building a durable foundation and ethical guardrails, so every future layer rests on something you can actually trust. ### From the Founder > *"My name is Travis J. Neuman, and Neuman Brain started as a personal line in the sand about how our tools should treat us.* > > *I've spent my career in information technology and operations. I've supported a multi-location hospitality group as an IT manager and senior systems administrator. I currently work as a technical project manager in managed services for a global industrial technology environment.* > > *Neuman Brain is not a protest against any employer - past or present. It is a response to something larger: the way we, as individuals and professionals, are increasingly bound to sprawling subscription stacks and unfinished products.* > > *What I stand for with Neuman Brain is simple:* > - *Tools that hold our lives together should be designed with respect, not manipulation* > - *Our information should not be hostage to subscriptions and shifting business models* > - *Foundations should be built carefully, even if that means progress looks slower from the outside* > > *I use this platform myself. If a decision wouldn't be acceptable for my own life, it doesn't belong in the product."* > > **- Travis J. Neuman** **[Read the full story in the app](https://brain.neuman.dev)** (Click "About" in the footer) --- ## Features ### Notes & Knowledge Management **Status:** Shipped (55% Obsidian parity, 50% Notion parity) | Feature | Status | Description | |---------|--------|-------------| | Rich Text Editor | ✅ Shipped | Lexical-powered editor with live preview | | Markdown Mode | ✅ Shipped | Full markdown editing support | | Folder Hierarchy | ✅ Shipped | Unlimited depth, drag-and-drop organization | | Full-Text Search | ✅ Shipped | Cmd/Ctrl+K quick switcher | | Auto-Save | ✅ Shipped | Debounced saves to IndexedDB | | Slash Commands | ✅ Shipped | /heading, /list, /code, /quote, /table | | Export to Markdown | ✅ Shipped | Download notes as .md files | | Word/Character Count | ✅ Shipped | Live status bar updates | | [[Wiki-links]] | 📋 Planned | Coming Q2 2026 | | Graph View | 💡 Future | Visual knowledge connections | --- ### Task & Project Management **Status:** Shipped (98% Jira/Linear parity) | Feature | Status | Description | |---------|--------|-------------| | Kanban Board | ✅ Shipped | Drag-and-drop tasks across columns | | Custom Columns | ✅ Shipped | Fully customizable workflow stages | | Subtasks | ✅ Shipped | Nested tasks with full properties | | Task Dependencies | ✅ Shipped | "Blocked By" and "Blocks" relationships | | List View | ✅ Shipped | Sortable columns, bulk operations | | Due Dates | ✅ Shipped | Calendar integration | | Priority Levels | ✅ Shipped | High/Medium/Low with visual indicators | | Export to CSV | ✅ Shipped | Full data export | | Gantt Charts | 📋 Planned | Timeline visualization | **Key Capabilities:** - Circular dependency prevention - Completion tracking with progress bars - Three-color badge system (green/blue/gray) - Bulk select, status change, priority change, delete - Card detail panel with rich editing --- ### Time Tracking **Status:** Shipped (74% Toggl parity) | Feature | Status | Description | |---------|--------|-------------| | Timer Controls | ✅ Shipped | Start/stop/pause/resume | | Sidebar Panel | ✅ Shipped | Toggl-style persistent timer | | **Timer Tab** | ✅ Shipped | Full-featured Schedule page tab | | Recent Entries | ✅ Shipped | 48-hour history with continue | | Manual Entry | ✅ Shipped | Add retroactive time entries | | Project Support | ✅ Shipped | Create, assign, filter by project | | Daily/Weekly Stats | ✅ Shipped | Visual charts and summaries | | Monthly Reports | ✅ Shipped | Heat map, project breakdown | | Calendar View | ✅ Shipped | Month grid, color-coded by project | | CSV Export | ✅ Shipped | RFC 4180 compliant | | Bulk Operations | ✅ Shipped | Multi-select, delete, update | | Billable Hours | 📋 Planned | Coming Phase 5 | **Timer Tab Features (New!):** - Synchronized with sidebar timer - Weekly statistics chart with daily breakdown - Today/weekly/daily average displays - Recent entries grid with click-to-continue --- ### Calendar & Scheduling **Status:** Shipped (65% Google Calendar parity) | Feature | Status | Description | |---------|--------|-------------| | Month/Week/Day Views | ✅ Shipped | Full calendar navigation | | Event Creation/Editing | ✅ Shipped | Rich event details | | Drag-and-Drop | ✅ Shipped | Reschedule events easily | | Recurring Events | ✅ Shipped | Daily, weekly, monthly patterns | | Multi-Day Events | ✅ Shipped | Visual representation | | Event Reminders | ✅ Shipped | Browser notifications | | ICS Import/Export | ✅ Shipped | Google Calendar compatible | | Agenda View | ✅ Shipped | Next 30 days list | | Conflict Detection | ✅ Shipped | Overlapping events warning | | Task Integration | ✅ Shipped | Due dates on calendar | | Day Detail Modal | ✅ Shipped | Click any day for full view | --- ### Dashboard & Widgets **Status:** Shipped (70% Notion Dashboard parity) **44+ widgets across 5 categories:** | Category | Widgets | Examples | |----------|---------|----------| | **Productivity** | 6 | TaskSummary, UpcomingEvents, RecentNotes, QuickAdd, Pomodoro | | **Information** | 9 | Weather, HackerNews, Reddit, DevTo, GitHub, Quotes | | **Utilities** | 14 | Calculator, QRCode, ColorPalette, Countdown, WorldClock | | **Finance** | 3 | Crypto, Currency, StockMarket | | **Media** | 1 | Unsplash (random beautiful images) | **Widget System Features:** - Drag-and-drop grid layout (react-grid-layout) - Resizable widgets - Widget settings/configuration - Lazy loading for performance - Two-tier storage persistence (IndexedDB + localStorage) --- ### AI Terminal **Status:** Shipped (8 AI providers, 200+ models) | Provider | Models | Features | |----------|--------|----------| | OpenRouter | 200+ | Free tier (50 req/day) | | Groq | LPU-powered | Ultra-fast inference | | HuggingFace | Open-source | Community models | | Mistral | European | GDPR-friendly | | Google Gemini | 1M context | Long documents | | OpenAI | GPT-4o, o1 | Latest models | | Anthropic | Claude 3.5 | Sonnet & Opus | | xAI | Grok | Real-time info | | **DeepSeek** | R1, V3 | Cost-effective reasoning | **Security Features:** - AES-256-GCM encrypted API key storage - Session-based password with expiry - Keys never logged or transmitted - Automatic provider fallback - Usage tracking per provider **Phantom Shell (Browser Terminal):** - Full xterm.js terminal emulator - Node.js in browser via WebContainers - AI ghost commands with `/ai` prefix - Live preview for dev servers --- ## Privacy & Security ### Zero-Data-Host Architecture | What | Where | You Control? | |------|-------|--------------| | Notes | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | | Tasks | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | | Calendar | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | | Time Entries | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | | Settings | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | | API Keys | Your device (encrypted) | 100% | ### Privacy Guarantees 1. **No data EVER leaves your device** - Everything stays in IndexedDB (50GB+ capacity) 2. **Zero tracking** - No Google Analytics, no Facebook Pixel, no ads 3. **No account required** - Use anonymously with local storage 4. **No cloud dependencies** - No Firebase, no Google Drive, no third-party servers 5. **Full data export always available** - Export to .brain files anytime 6. **Open source** - Audit the code yourself ### Analytics (Optional) We use **Cloudflare Web Analytics** for basic, anonymous site metrics: - Privacy-focused (no cookies, no fingerprinting) - GDPR/CCPA compliant - IP addresses never stored - No individual user tracking **[Read full Privacy Policy](https://brain.neuman.dev)** (Click "Privacy & Terms" in the footer) --- ## Comparison with Alternatives | Feature | Neuman Brain | Notion | Obsidian | Monday.com | Toggl | |---------|--------------|--------|----------|------------|-------| | **Cost** | Free | $10-15/mo | Free (Sync $10) | $12-20/mo | Free-$18/mo | | **Data Privacy** | Local-first | Cloud-only | Local files | Cloud-only | Cloud-only | | **Platform** | Web, PWA | Web, apps | Desktop-first | Web, apps | Web, apps | | **Offline** | Full | Limited | Full | No | Limited | | **Notes + Tasks** | Integrated | Separate | Plugin-based | Weak notes | No notes | | **Time Tracking** | Built-in | No | Plugin | Partial | Core | | **Bundle Size** | 117KB | 2-4MB | Fast | 3MB+ | N/A | | **Open Source** | Yes (MIT) | No | Source-available | No | No | ### Feature Parity | Category | Competitor | Parity | |----------|------------|--------| | Notes | Obsidian | 55% | | Notes | Notion | 50% | | Tasks/PM | Jira/Linear | 98% | | Time Tracking | Toggl | 74% | | Calendar | Google Calendar | 65% | | Dashboard | Notion | 70% | --- ## Getting Started ### For Users **No installation required!** Just visit: **[https://brain.neuman.dev](https://brain.neuman.dev)** Your data is stored locally in your browser. No account needed. **Quick Start:** 1. Visit the app 2. Start creating notes, tasks, or events 3. Track your time with the sidebar timer or Timer tab 4. Explore the 44+ dashboard widgets 5. Export your data anytime (Settings → Backup & Sync) --- ### For Developers **Prerequisites:** - Node.js 22+ (see `.node-version`) - npm or pnpm **Setup:** ```bash # Clone repository git clone https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase.git cd system.knowledgebase # Install dependencies npm install # Start development server npm run dev # Open http://localhost:5173 # Run tests npm run test # Type checking npm run type-check # Build for production npm run build ``` **Developer Workflow:** We use a task-based workflow documented in `CLAUDE.md`: 1. **Before coding:** Create task file in `tasks/[feature-name].md` 2. **During work:** Update task file with progress 3. **Git hooks:** Pre-commit hook blocks commits without task files 4. **After work:** Add review section, archive completed tasks **Key Files:** - `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow guide with 12 core rules - `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Step-by-step checklist - `.claude/templates/task-template.md` - Task file template --- ## Architecture & Tech Stack ### Frontend | Technology | Version | Purpose | |------------|---------|---------| | React | 19 | UI framework | | TypeScript | 5.9 | Type safety (strict mode) | | Vite | 7 | Build tool, HMR | | Zustand | 5 | State management (1KB) | | Tailwind CSS | 3 | Styling | | Vitest | - | Testing | ### Data Storage (Local-First) | Layer | Technology | Capacity | |-------|------------|----------| | Primary | IndexedDB | 50GB+ | | Backups | .brain files | Unlimited | | Auto-save | File System Access API | User folder | ### Performance | Metric | Value | |--------|-------| | Bundle Size | 117KB gzipped | | Initial Load | < 1s | | Code Splitting | 9 feature-based chunks | | Lazy Loading | Widgets, Calendar, Kanban, AI | ### Project Structure ``` system.knowledgebase/ ├── src/ │ ├── pages/ # Dashboard, Tasks, Notes, TimeTracking, Settings │ ├── widgets/ # 44+ dashboard widgets │ ├── components/ # Reusable UI components │ ├── stores/ # Zustand state management │ ├── services/ # Data persistence, backup, AI │ └── types/ # TypeScript definitions ├── docs/ # Documentation (56 files) ├── tasks/ # Active development tasks ├── .archive/ # Completed tasks, historical docs └── .claude/ # Automation tools, templates ``` --- ## Roadmap ### Phase 0: Foundation ✅ Complete - React/TypeScript rebuild - Kanban, Calendar, Time Tracking - Privacy-first local storage - AI Terminal with 8+ providers ### Phase 1: Notes & PKM ✅ Complete - Lexical rich text editor - Folders, tags, search - Slash commands, markdown - Export to Markdown ### Phase 2: Stability & Polish (Current) - Modal overflow fixes - Timer Tab in Schedule page - Calendar day detail modal - Notes sidebar resize ### Phase 3: Habit Tracking & Gamification (Q1 2026) - Daily habits with streaks - XP, levels, badges - Analytics and visualizations ### Phase 4: Advanced PM (Q2 2026) - Gantt charts - Resource management - Billable time tracking ### Phase 5: Collaboration (Q2-Q3 2026) - Real-time sync - Comments & @mentions - Shared workspaces **[Full Roadmap](docs/product/feature_roadmap.md)** --- ## Contributing We welcome contributions! Neuman Brain is open source (MIT License). ### How to Contribute 1. **Report bugs:** [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues) 2. **Request features:** [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/discussions) 3. **Submit code:** Fork repo → Create feature branch → Submit PR 4. **Improve docs:** Documentation PRs are especially welcome! ### Development Guidelines - **TypeScript:** Strict mode, no `any` types - **Tests:** Add tests for new features (Vitest + Testing Library) - **Code Style:** Follow existing patterns, run `npm run lint` - **Documentation:** Update relevant docs in `/docs` - **Task Files:** Create task file before coding (see CLAUDE.md) **[See CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)** for detailed guidelines. --- ## Business Model ### How is this free? **No catch.** Neuman Brain has **zero hosting costs**: - Static hosting (Cloudflare Pages free tier) - No backend servers - No database hosting fees - Users store their own data locally ### Sustainability Plans | Model | Description | Status | |-------|-------------|--------| | Donations | GitHub Sponsors, Open Collective | Planned | | Client-side Pro | Advanced local features (your API key) | Future | | Professional Services | Custom integrations, training | Available | **Commitment:** Core functionality will **always be free**. **[Full Business Model](docs/business/business_model.md)** --- ## Legal ### Privacy Policy We respect your privacy. All data stays on YOUR device. We use Cloudflare Web Analytics (privacy-focused, no cookies) for basic site metrics. **[Read full Privacy Policy](https://brain.neuman.dev)** (Click "Privacy & Terms" in the footer) ### Terms & Conditions Currently being drafted. Core principles: - Your data, your control - Full data portability - No hidden agendas ### License **MIT License** - Use for anything, including commercial projects. See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for full terms. --- ## Acknowledgments ### Built With | Technology | Purpose | |------------|---------| | [React](https://react.dev/) | UI framework | | [TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org/) | Type safety | | [Vite](https://vitejs.dev/) | Build tool | | [Zustand](https://github.com/pmndrs/zustand) | State management | | [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/) | Styling | | [Lexical](https://lexical.dev/) | Rich text editor | | [Vitest](https://vitest.dev/) | Testing | | [Leaflet](https://leafletjs.com/) | Maps | | [Open-Meteo](https://open-meteo.com/) | Weather API | ### Inspired By - **Notion** - All-in-one flexibility - **Obsidian** - Local-first, powerful linking - **Apple Notes** - Simplicity and speed - **Linear** - Professional task management - **Toggl** - Elegant time tracking - **Habitica** - Gamification for productivity --- ## Contact | Channel | Link | |---------|------| | **Live App** | [brain.neuman.dev](https://brain.neuman.dev) | | **GitHub** | [github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase) | | **Issues** | [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues) | | **Discussions** | [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/discussions) | | **Email** | [brain@neuman.dev](mailto:brain@neuman.dev) | ---
### Star This Repo! If you find Neuman Brain useful, please star the repository on GitHub! It helps others discover the project. [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase?style=social)](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/stargazers) --- **Made with care for privacy, productivity, and open source** **Your data. Your device. Your brain.** --- *Last updated: November 26, 2025*
